tweet: Climate change is the biggest existential threat of our lifetime. An overwhelming majority of Americans want #ClimateAction, so why aren't we doing more?  Because @SenateGOP uses an arcane, Jim Crow era relic to stop the will of a democratically elected majority #EndTheFilibuster

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: The filibuster allows a minority of Senators to prevent overwhelmingly popular legislation from becoming law. It is undemocratic and must be abolished #EndTheFilibuster

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: Now, the filibuster is being used by Senate Republicans to prevent:  - Meaningful action on climate change  - Voting rights legislation  - Steps to curb gun violence

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: In 1917, the filibuster was first used by white supremacists to prevent a bill that would have made lynching a crime. In the Jim Crow era, the filibuster was only used to block civil rights legislation.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: The rules in the Senate are the same as they are in the House. In order to pass a bill, a simple majority must vote in favor of the bill.   Where the Senate differs is that, with the filibuster, you need a supermajority to even bring a bill to a vote.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: It’s time to #EndTheFilibuster. It’s an arcane, Jim Crow era relic that is preventing a democratically elected majority from passing overwhelmingly popular legislation on a range of issues.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: Republicans proposed at least 250 voting restriction laws at the state level. Georgia has already passed a voter suppression bill.  H.R. 1 is waiting to be passed in the Senate & it would protect our right to vote.   This is an urgent crisis. We must #EndTheFilibuster & pass it.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: We’re facing public health, economic, and climate crises. We’re in the midst of a racial justice reckoning.   We can't afford to let Republicans in the Senate block the People's Agenda. Lives are on the line. I joined @RepCori in calling on the Senate to #EndTheFilibuster.

tweet: When Adam Smith wrote about the power of market capitalism’s “invisible hand” to adequately ensure a fair and efficient exchange of goods and services, he probably didn’t envision the U.S. government subsidizing a sector that’s seen prices decline by 72 % in the last decade.

name: Rep. Sean Casten

tweet: We need to be doing everything possible to fight this coronavirus.   President Trump should do the right thing, fund the government, give us the tools to fight this pandemic, and then call on the @HouseGOP to stop blocking struggling families from receiving #COVIDrelief.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: All these steps were absolutely, completely necessary. And they also weren’t even close to enough.   They were a downpayment on the action we have to take in the coming years – but I’m beyond excited to continue the fight next year with @JoeBiden & his team of climate all-stars.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: And that wasn’t all.   The bill also provided for roughly $35 billion total in clean energy R&D for other energy technologies, and extended clean energy tax provisions that we desperately need.  https://t.co/gFPWWP5IEG

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: But there were other massively important energy provisions in yesterday’s bill.   A proposal to reduce hydrofluorocarbons was included, which by itself will prevent half a degree Celsius of global warming.   Hard to overstate what a big deal that is!

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: 2️⃣Our #FY21 package also includes my bill with @RepBillFoster directing $855 million to fund energy storage R&D and demonstration programs at DoE.    Energy storage is vital to decarbonizing our electricity grid & I’m incredibly proud to see these two bills poised to become law.  https://t.co/ycoQLiJcQG

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: Industrial emissions represented roughly 22% of all the GHG’s we pumped into the atmosphere last year.   In order to get to net-zero emissions by 2050, we have to figure out a way to eliminate these emissions – this bill will help us develop the tech to do just that!

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: First, I want to highlight two bills I helped lead:  1️⃣ The Clean Industrial Technology Act authorizes $500 million in R&D funding to figure out how to decarbonize hard-to-decarbonize industries like steel or cement production.  https://t.co/Dr3LvzyucR

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: This pandemic is a case study on why policymakers should always put science before politics.   That’s why in addition to providing desperately needed #COVIDrelief, the #FY21 omnibus includes important provisions in our fight against the climate crisis!   A not-so-brief 🧵

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: Helping our small businesses survive is NOT fiscally irresponsible.  Ensuring Americans who lost their jobs  due to the pandemic can keep food on the table is NOT fiscally irresponsible.  Doing nothing to control a virus that’s killed 318,000 Americans is fiscally irresponsible.

name: Rep. Sean Casten

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: HFCs are a potent class of greenhouse gases used in air conditioners and refrigerators.   Our #COVIDagreement’s HFC provision will phase down HFCs 85% over 15 years—preventing half a degree Celsius in global warming.   This is a BIG deal 👇🏼

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: Bottom line, #ClimateAction is as much about saving our economy as it is about saving our planet.   Trump’s anti-science agenda has cost the American people & our economy immeasurable devastation.   We CANNOT fail to protect our financial system against the #ClimateCrisis.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: Together, my common-sense #ClimateRisk bills will:   ➡️Require companies to be transparent about their exposures   ➡️Mitigate risk and shine a light on the enormous threat to our financial system posed by #ClimateChange   ➡️Kickstart our transition to a #CleanEnergyEconomy

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: 1️⃣ We should pass the Climate Change Financial Risk Act—legislation I introduced with @brianschatz requiring the Fed to stress test #ClimateRisk.   2️⃣ We should pass the Climate Risk Disclosure Act—my bill with @SenWarren requiring public companies to disclose climate risks.

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: Failing to take sufficient action on #ClimateRisk will result in devastation we cannot afford right now.   But by tackling the @ClimateCrisis head-on, we will protect public health, save taxpayer dollars & safeguard our economy as we begin to rebuild  Here’s two next steps ⬇️

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: 2️⃣ The #ClimateCrisis is also changing long-term climate patterns in ways that will:  ➡️Lower labor productivity   ➡️Devalue and destroy fixed assets   ➡️Stress agricultural yields   ➡️Ultimately impact every sector of our economy

name: Rep. Sean Casten
tweet: 1️⃣Through rising sea levels, forest fires, increasingly frequent extreme weather, water shortages & resource scarcity, the #ClimateCrisis directly threatens valuable assets.   https://t.co/aqIxZ3fiW7
